overview: This step simplifies the addition or removal of inter-segment whitespace when translating to or from Chinese or Japanese scripts that do not typically use spaces. When translating from a space-delimited language to a non-space-delimited language, whitespace following segment-ending punctuation is removed; in the reverse direction, whitespace is added. The step also converts between full-width and standard punctuation forms (e.g., Ideographic Full Stop U+3002 to/from period). It performs no action when translating between two space-delimited languages or between Chinese and Japanese.

punctuation.description: Comma-separated list of punctuation classes to which the whitespace correction should be applied. Each class controls both whitespace adjustment and punctuation form conversion between full-width/ideographic and standard forms. Valid values are: FULL_STOP (converts Ideographic Full Stop U+3002 and Full-width Full Stop U+FF0E to/from a period), COMMA (converts Ideographic Comma U+3001 and Full-width Comma U+FF0C to/from a comma), EXCLAMATION_MARK (converts Full-width Exclamation Mark U+FF01 to/from an exclamation point), QUESTION_MARK (converts Full-width Question Mark U+FF1F to/from a question mark). By default all four punctuation classes are enabled.

whitespace.description: Comma-separated list of Unicode whitespace character names that the step considers as whitespace for removal or addition operations. The default value includes a comprehensive set of Unicode whitespace characters: standard spaces (SPACE, NO_BREAK_SPACE), line breaks (LINE_FEED, CARRIAGE_RETURN, NEXT_LINE, LINE_SEPARATOR, PARAGRAPH_SEPARATOR), tabs (CHARACTER_TABULATION, LINE_TABULATION), form feeds (FORM_FEED), typographic spaces (EN_QUAD, EM_QUAD, EN_SPACE, EM_SPACE, THREE_PER_EM_SPACE, FOUR_PER_EM_SPACE, SIX_PER_EM_SPACE, FIGURE_SPACE, PUNCUATION_SPACE, THIS_SPACE, HAIR_SPACE, NARROW_NO_BREAK_SPACE, MEDIUM_MATHEMATICAL_SPACE), ideographic space (IDEOGRAPHIC_SPACE), and zero-width characters (ZERO_WIDTH_SPACE, ZERO_WIDTH_NON_BREAKING_SPACE).

limitations: Relies on the assumption that each source segment contains a single sentence and has been translated to a single sentence in the target language, so results may be incorrect for multi-sentence segments.
Performs no action when translating between two space-delimited languages (e.g., English to French) or between Chinese and Japanese.
processingNotes: The step operates on filter events (takes filter events, sends filter events) and processes text units.
The step requires both SOURCE_LOCALE and TARGET_LOCALE to determine whether to add or remove whitespace based on whether each locale uses space-delimited script.
When translating from a space-delimited to a non-space-delimited language, whitespace following segment-ending punctuation is removed.
When translating from a non-space-delimited to a space-delimited language, whitespace is added following segment-ending punctuation.
In addition to whitespace adjustment, the step converts punctuation between full-width/ideographic forms and standard ASCII forms as appropriate for the target locale.

examples.title: English to Chinese translation whitespace removal
examples.description: When translating from English (space-delimited) to Chinese (non-space-delimited), the step removes whitespace after segment-ending punctuation and converts standard punctuation to full-width/ideographic equivalents. For example, a period followed by a space would become an Ideographic Full Stop (U+3002) with no trailing space.

examples.title: Chinese to English translation whitespace addition
examples.description: When translating from Chinese (non-space-delimited) to English (space-delimited), the step adds whitespace after segment-ending punctuation and converts full-width/ideographic punctuation to standard ASCII equivalents. For example, an Ideographic Full Stop (U+3002) would become a period followed by a space.
